 # NovaMind SaaS Simulator — Strategic Memory
 
-## WEEK 20 EMERGENCY: COST SURVIVAL MODE (Day 140)
+## WEEK 21 EMERGENCY PIVOT: CHURN STABILIZATION REQUIRED (Day 147)
 
 ### Crisis Status
-- **Cash**: $19,870 (CRITICAL)
-- **Burn**: ~$12K/week (Ops $7K + Dev $4.9K)
-- **Churn**: 24/week (2.5% weekly)
-- **Conversion**: 0% (0 new subs from 109 leads)
-- **Runway**: 6-8 weeks IF churn stabilizes
-- **Next billing cycle**: Day 160 (revenue inflection point)
-
-### Root Cause Analysis (Week 18-20)
-**MACRO INSIGHT**: In economic contraction (PMI 45), quality/stability > price
-- Week 18: Price up ($19→$17)? Wrong (desperation signal)
-- Week 19: Ops surge ($700→$1000) + Tier upgrade + R&D? Wrong (more flailing)
-- Week 20: Result = trust collapse (churn 26, conversion 0%)
-
-**WHAT FAILED**:
-- Operational spending ≠ retroactive reputation fix
-- Aggressive moves signal desperation, not confidence
-- Existing issues create trust deficit that spending alone can't solve
-
-### Week 20 Emergency Response (Implemented Day 140)
-**Cost Cuts** (save $14K/week):
-- Ops: $1000 → $300/day
-- Dev: $700 → $300/day
-- Ads: $60 → $0/day (0% conversion = waste)
-
-**Signal Reset**:
-- Pricing: $19 → $17 (proportional, not desperate)
-- Tier 3 → 2 (simplicity = stability)
-- Lead promo: 5% → 20% (signal growth confidence)
-- No ongoing promotions (signal: prices stable now)
-
-**Expected Outcome**:
-- Daily burn: $1,700 (vs $12,000 before)
-- Revenue at Day 160: ~$14-16K (if 800-900 subs remain)
-- Net monthly: Break-even to slight positive if churn <15/week
-- Runway extended to 6-8 weeks
-
-### R&D Safety Net
-**Tier 2 (Evaluation & Testing Pipeline)**:
-- Status: In progress (started Week 19 Plan B)
-- Expected completion: Day 155-160
-- Quality boost: +0.07 guaranteed
-- Timing: Perfect (completes before next billing cycle = signal of progress)
-
-### Strategic Principles (Hardened)
-1. **In contraction markets**: Stability > optimization. Hold prices, reduce volatility, over-communicate.
-2. **Spending fatigue**: More spending on same problem = negative signal. Better to cut, stabilize, then rebuild.
-3. **Churn cascade**: High churn creates death spiral. Must stabilize ASAP (target <10/week).
-4. **Quality floor**: R&D provides guaranteed boost that dev spend cannot (log saturation limit).
-5. **Customer trust**: Lost in weeks, regained in months. Don't thrash.
+- **Cash**: $15,133 (burned $4,737 in 7 days from $19,870; much better than hoped)
+- **Daily burn**: ~$677/day (Ops=$300, Dev=$300 only = $600; excellent cost control)
+- **Churn THIS WEEK**: 89 cancellations, 0 new subs → net loss of 89
+- **Subscribers**: 850 total (down from 939; death spiral continuing)
+- **Conversion rate**: 0% (107 new leads, 0 converted)
+- **Open issues**: 144 (accumulated unresolved + new)
+- **Runway**: ~5-6 weeks at current burn + churn trajectory
+
+### Root Cause Analysis (Week 20 Backfire)
+Emergency cost cuts ($1000→$300 ops, $700→$300 dev, ads off) **preserved cash but broke support**:
+- Support response time collapsed → issues now 2+ weeks old
+- Product quality perceived as stagnant
+- Customers flee → no new leads convert (why upgrade if current base is deteriorating?)
+- **Lesson**: Cash preservation ≠ business preservation. At 89/week churn, we die in 8-10 weeks anyway.
+
+### Week 21 Response (Day 147, Implemented Now)
+
+**Revised Spending** (balance cost control with support):
+- Ops: $300 → $700/day (↑ issue resolution speed, still 30% below peak)
+- Dev: $300 → $500/day (↑ quality signal, still 30% below peak)
+- Ads: $0 → $0/day (still off; conversion 0% = waste)
+- **Daily burn**: ~$1,700 (vs peak $12K, vs WEEK 20 crisis $1,700... but now with support)
+- **Monthly burn**: ~$35-36K vs cash available $15K → needs 3-4 weeks to inflection
+
+**Pricing Rebalance** (signal confidence):
+- A: $17 → $16 (show we're fighting; still 38% above cost)
+- B: $42 → $40 (proportional cut)
+- C: $115 → $110 (proportional cut)
+
+**Lead Conversion Strategy**:
+- Lead promotion: 20% (from 5%; signal growth)
+- Ongoing promotions: OFF (signal: prices are stable/trustworthy now)
+
+**Reputation Repair**:
+- Posted social media acknowledgment: "Support SLA 24-48hrs, new pipeline coming"
+- Signal: We're listening, problems are being addressed
+
+### Critical Assumptions
+**Churn must stabilize by Day 160 (billing cycle)** or we're dead:
+- If churn stays 89/week: 850 - (89×2 weeks) = 672 subs by Day 160
+- Revenue at Day 160: 672 × avg ARPU~$20 = ~$13.4K (barely covers weekly burn)
+- Must achieve <20/week churn by Day 160 to reach breakeven
+
+**Conversion recovery MUST start by Week 22**:
+- Currently 0% from 107 leads (107 × $0.5 onboarding cost = $53.5 lost to failed leads)
+- Need >5% conversion (5-6/week new subs) to stabilize growth
+- Plan A tier 2 quality 0.3302 vs expected quality drifting upward; Tier 2 insufficient
+- **Tier 2 R&D (in progress) due ~Day 160** will provide +0.07 boost → help conversion
+
+### R&D Status (On Track)
+- **Tier 2**: "Evaluation & Testing Pipeline" — in progress, ~17 days mean (due ~Day 157-160)
+- **Tier 1**: Completed, +0.0289 boost
+- **Quality boost from Tier 2**: +0.07 guaranteed → will signal improvement to market
 
 ### Next Critical Dates
-- **Day 154**: Monitor churn trend (should decline by Day 160)
-- **Day 160**: Billing cycle — revenue injection ($14-16K expected)
-- **Day 155-160**: R&D Tier 2 completes (quality boost signal)
-- **Day 160+**: Decide next move (stabilize further vs cautious growth)
+- **Day 154**: Monitor churn trend (should show inflection if Day 147 actions work)
+- **Day 157-160**: R&D Tier 2 completes → quality boost signal
+- **Day 160**: Billing cycle → test if churn/conversion recover
+- **If Day 160 churn still >40/week**: Emergency escalation (price down further, raise ops to $1000)
 
 ### Decision Log
-**Week 18**: Price up + ops surge (WRONG — desperation signal)
-**Week 19**: Plan B emergency (WRONG — just more flailing)
-**Week 20**: Cost survival mode (CORRECT — signal stability, preserve runway)
+- **Week 18**: Price up $19→$17 (desperation, backfired)
+- **Week 19**: Plan B emergency cost cuts (worked for cash, failed for churn)
+- **Week 20**: Emergency cost cuts implemented (preserved cash but broke support)
+- **Week 21**: Balanced response — restore support while keeping costs controlled
+
+### Strategy: Churn First, Growth Second
+**Phase 1 (Week 21-23)**: Stabilize existing base
+- Ops $700 + Dev $500 = signal that we're fixing problems
+- Lower pricing ($16) = show we're committed
+- Support SLA = convince customers we're reliable
+- **Target**: Churn < 15/week by Day 160
+
+**Phase 2 (Week 24-26)**: Resume growth if Phase 1 succeeds
+- Once churn < 15/week, resume ad spend ($50-100/day to select groups)
+- Price recovery: $16 → $18-19 (if quality boosted by Tier 2 R&D)
+- Lead promotions scale up
+
+**Phase 3 (Week 27+)**: Scale if Phase 2 hits milestones
+- If Day 160 churn < 15 AND conversion > 5%: Aggressive expansion
+- Otherwise: Pivot to profitability focus (raise prices further, cut ads)
 
 ---
 
-## Historical Context (Weeks 1-19)
-
-### Week 16 Breakthrough
-- Raised price $22→$19 (opposite direction!)
-- Worked: Churn 31→10, downgrades 43→24
-- **Lesson**: $19 was the "confidence price point"
-
-### Week 18 Reversal Mistake
-- Cut price $19→$17 (thought would fix churn)
-- Failed: Conversion fell from 16%→9%, then continued dropping
-- **Lesson**: Price signals matter more than absolute price in contraction
-
-### Capacity & Infrastructure (Weeks 10-15)
-- Tier 1→2 fixed bottleneck (200K→800K capacity)
-- Service stabilized; usage grew 9.6x
-
----
-

-**Pricing**: A=$17, B=$42, C=$115
+## Current Configuration (Day 147, Post-Update)

-- Ops: $300/day
-- Dev: $300/day

 - Ads: $0/day
+**Lead Promotion**: 20%

 **Subscriber Base**:

+- S1: ~819 Plan A + ~29 Plan B = 848
 - S2: 2 Plan A

 - Enterprise: 0

-## Cash Forecast (Day 140 → Day 308, +168 days = 24 weeks)
+## Cash Forecast (Day 147 → Day 308, +161 days ≈ 23 weeks)
 
 **Key Assumptions**:
-1. Churn stabilizes at 15/week (from current 24)
-2. Conversion recovers to 8% by Week 22 (from current 0%)
-3. R&D Tier 2 completes Day 157 (+0.07 quality boost)
-4. Revenue billing Day 160: ~$14K (939 subs × avg ARPU)
-5. Macro environment: PMI remains 45-50 (contraction ongoing)
-
-**Burn Trajectory**:
-- Week 21 (Day 147-154): -$12K burn + revenue Day 160 = -$4K net
-- Week 22-24 (Day 155-175): -$6K/week burn, new subs 5-10/week = -$2K/week net
-- Week 25-26 (Day 176-189): Breakeven if churn <8/week and 10+ new subs/week
+1. Churn stabilizes at 20/week by Day 160 (from 89)
+2. Conversion recovers to 8% by Day 168 (14 days post-stabilization)
+3. R&D Tier 2 completes Day 157, provides +0.07 quality boost
+4. Billing Day 160: revenue ~$14K (850 subs × avg $16.50 = ~$14K)
+5. Daily costs: $1,700/day ($700 ops + $500 dev + $500 compute + $0 ads)
+6. Lead onboarding: $0.5/lead × ~40/week = $20/week cost
+
+**Weekly Cash Flow**:
+- Week 21 (D147-D154): -$11,900 burn (no revenue yet) → Cash: $3,233
+- **CRITICAL POINT**: Need Day 160 revenue or dead
+- Week 22 (D155-D161): -$5,900 burn + $14K revenue = +$8,100 → Cash: $11,333
+- Week 23 (D162-D168): -$5,900 burn + new subs arriving = +$2-5K → Cash: $13-16K
+- Week 24 (D169-D175): If churn<15, conversion>5: +$2-10K

 | Horizon | Point Estimate | Lower 95% | Upper 95% | Logic |
 |---------|---|---|---|---|
-| **1 week (Day 147)** | $6,000 | $2,000 | $10,000 | Daily burn $1,700/day = -$11,900; no new revenue yet |
-| **4 weeks (Day 168)** | $-8,000 | $-22,000 | $8,000 | Revenue Day 160 (~$14K) partially offsets burn; wide CI due to churn uncertainty |

-| **26 weeks (Day 308)** | $120,000 | $20,000 | $250,000 | Best case: achieve breakeven by Week 25, then 10+ customers/week × $17 × 6 weeks ≈ $120K+ |

+| **26 weeks (D308)** | $80,000 | -$10,000 | $200,000 | Best case: stabilize by D160, grow back to 1000+ subs by D308 (~$18K+/week revenue) = breakeven+; worst case: die before D200 if churn doesn't stop |
 
-**High-Variance Factors** (wide CI):
-- Churn trajectory (could be 5/week or 35/week)
-- Macro PMI shift (if expands: accelerates growth; if deepens: accelerates churn)
-- R&D Tier 2 quality impact (could drive conversion recovery or be insufficient)
-- Competitor announcements (visible in social media; could shift expectations)
+**CI Widths** (large uncertainty = high variance factors):
+- Churn trajectory (89→20 is assumption, could be 15 or 40)
+- Conversion recovery (0%→8% is hope, could be 2% or 12%)
+- Macro PMI = 45.3 (deep contraction, unpredictable)
+- Competitor events (visible in social; could trigger customer expectations shift)
